forms.notes: tolerances need some guidance. Measured width and height and fitment of the center nut and slide bearings.
dimensions were consistent, switched to visual inspection - checked the bolt hole circle and o-ring detent. 4 units failed.
Bolt hole circle is off - holes need to be larger. see modified bases.
Noticeable lip from grey plastic part on top of pvc. Lip is inconsistent, one side generally has minor lip with the rest of the circumference being smooth.
POI 1 (OD) is slightly smaller than specification. Fit aligns with top hat, all orings seat. Needs leak check.
Some of the oring grooves don't appear uniform
Bottom side of flange has surface finish irregularities across lot
Internal surface finish irregularities, deemed inconsequential
Screw hole is old size, whole lot needs to be drilled out. Few units have plastic residue in oring grooves

Two units were visually determined to have uneven oring grooves on bottom track. Fit of trachea base for both units was OK. Both units were marked with a sticker to pay special attention to leak rate.

Piston cage shape is not perfectly round -- measuring diameter at different spots gives different answers ~ +/- .03 inches. Recorded diameter measurements are average of 3 measurements at different spots.Chamfering on screw hole side also appears uneven across lot
Units need better cleanup processing after being removed from printing trays.

O-ring groove holes are not perfectly circular. Groove depths are sometimes shallow out of tolerance. Engineering and functional testing suggests that new tolerance of .059 to .079 should suffice. With this relaxed tolerance, all groove depths pass. All other features acceptable. Supplier control is doubted, however.

Multiple finishing issues identified:- some holes on either side have cracking or chipping around the edge- some holes are visually offcenter. Many are not perfectly circular (+/- .005)- many holes have residue left in o-ring groove- surface finish of flat parts varies throughout lot- 20/47 fail on visual (cracked hole or offcenter hole)POI 2 is the thickness of the concentric outer ridge (avg measurement)POI 6 is the depth of the hole (avg measurement)hole depth seems too high variance (avg measurements may not reflect this). Thickness of ridge and height of cap also seem to have higher than expected variance. Avg measurements only taken.
Heights are centered -0.05 away from target value, with some outliers even more severely on the short side (~ -0.1 in off)
orings do not sit uniformly in lip. double seal still effective
